name: Lemon Snowflake Cake - Country Living
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Lemon Filling: In a 2-quart saucepan, combine sugar, cornstarch, and salt until well mixed. Gradually add water, stirring until smooth. Heat mixture to boiling over medium heat, stirring constantly with a wire whisk; boil for 1 minute (or until the mixture thickens). In a small bowl, quickly stir some of the hot mixture into egg yolks, then stir the egg-yolk mixture into the remaining hot mixture in the saucepan until well blended. Reduce heat to low and add butter, cooking the mixture for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat; stir in lemon juice and rind. Cool to room temperature and cover the surface of the filling with plastic wrap. Refrigerate until completely cold, at least 2 hours.
2. Prepare Cake Layers: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease three 9-inch round baking pans. Line the bottoms of the pans with parchment paper or waxed paper, greasing and flouring the paper. In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt.
3. In a large bowl, beat 1 3/4 cups sugar until very light and fluffy. Beat in lemon juice, lemon rind, and vanilla extract. Alternately add the flour mixture with milk to the butter mixture, beginning and ending with the flour mixture, beating until smooth batter forms.
4. In another bowl, beat egg whites and remaining 1/4 cup sugar until stiff peaks form. Gently fold the egg whites into the batter using a rubber spatula. Divide evenly among prepared pans.
5. Bake for 25 minutes or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool cake layers in pans on wire racks for 10 minutes, then invert onto wire racks and remove parchment paper. Allow layers to cool completely.
6. Prepare White-Chocolate Snowflakes: Melt 1 1/2 bars white chocolate over hot, not boiling water or in a microwave. Place the melted chocolate in a pastry bag fitted with a writing tip. Reserve the pan used for melting. On an aluminum foil-lined sheet, pipe twenty-eight snowflakes (about 2 inches each). Decorate with silver dragees if desired. Place the sheet of snowflakes in the freezer until the cake is frosted.
7. Prepare White-Chocolate Frosting: Melt remaining 2 1/2 bars white chocolate in the same pan; allow it to cool to room temperature. In a large bowl, beat the cream cheese until smooth. Gradually beat in the white chocolate, butter, and lemon juice until the frosting is smooth.
8. Assemble Cake: Trim tops of cake layers to make them level. Place one cake layer on a serving plate and spread with half of the lemon filling. Repeat with the second cake layer and filling. Place the last cake layer upside down on top of the filling to complete the 3-layer cake. Frost the cake smoothly with White-Chocolate Frosting. Remove snowflakes from the freezer, gently peel them from the foil, and press a few into the side of the cake. Pile the remaining snowflakes on top of the cake. Serve immediately or store in the refrigerator.
